title: Tanker report - Week 24
summary:
VLCCs
A rather lackluster start to the week in the Middle East Gulf, saw rates initially hold at around WS 50/52 region basis 270,000 tonnes going long/short east respectively. However, as the week progressed renewed pressure from charterers led to further rate erosion with 270,000 tonnes to South Korea fixed at WS 47.5, with WS 48.5 agreed for China while CPC fixed 265,000 tonnes to Taiwan at WS 49.5. West rates have held steady at around WS 26 cape/cape for 280,000 tonnes.

children.title: VLCCs
children.summary: A rather lackluster start to the week in the Middle East Gulf, saw rates initially hold at around WS 50/52 region basis 270,000 tonnes going long/short east respectively. However, as the week progressed renewed pressure from charterers led to further rate erosion with 270,000 tonnes to South Korea fixed at WS 47.5, with WS 48.5 agreed for China while CPC fixed 265,000 tonnes to Taiwan at WS 49.5.

children.title: Suezmaxes
children.summary: A more active week in West Africa did little more than take out some of the surplus tonnage and with charterers still having plenty of choice, rates have been steady in the low WS 60s to UKC-Med, in contrast to the WS 65 seen at the end of last week. There is rather a sense of déjà vu with rates for 135,000 tonnes in the Black Sea seemingly settled at around WS 72.5 region, with South Korea discharge paying around $2.55 million.

children.title: Aframaxes
children.summary: Although there were healthy levels of enquiry in the Mediterranean at the start of the week, rates remained largely unchanged with the market for 80,000 tonnes sitting at around WS 95 level. In the Baltic, by contrast, rates for 100,000 tonnes to UKCont recovered from the WS 67.5 seen a week ago to WS 75, as plenty of enquiry early in the week helped thin the tonnage list.

children.title: Panamaxes
children.summary: Healthy amounts of enquiry provided cover for some of the ballasters and rates for 55,000 tonnes ARA to US Gulf have nudged up 2.5 points to WS 115 level, with Skikda paying similar levels.

children.title: Clean
children.summary: Rates in the Middle East Gulf for 75,000 tonnes to Japan initially held at between WS 87.5 and WS 90 region before coming under renewed downward pressure with the market now assessed at closer to WS 85 while in the 55,000 tonnes AG/Japan trade rates have been steady at around WS 105 level. In the 37,000 tonnes Cont/USAC trade, it has been a disappointing week for owners.
